data.question: What is the most expensive cake and its flavor?

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor, Price FROM goods WHERE Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM goods)

data.question: What is the cheapest cookie and its flavor?

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor, Price FROM goods WHERE Price = (SELECT MIN(Price) FROM goods)

data.question: Find the ids of goods that have apple flavor.

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Flavor) = LOWER('apple')

data.question: What are the ids of goods that cost less than 3 dollars?

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE Price < 3

data.question: List the distinct ids of all customers who bought a lemon cake?

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.CustomerId FROM items AS T1 INNER JOIN receipts AS T2 ON T1.Receipt = T2.ReceiptNumber INNER JOIN goods AS T3 ON T1.Item = T3.Id WHERE LOWER(T3.Flavor) = LOWER('lemon cake')

data.question: For each type of food, tell me how many customers have ever bought it.

prediction.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT i.CustomerId) AS count FROM items i INNER JOIN receipts r ON i.Receipt = r.ReceiptNumber INNER JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id GROUP BY g.Food

data.question: Find the id of customers who shopped at the bakery at least 15 times.

prediction.sql: SELECT CustomerId FROM receipts GROUP BY CustomerId HAVING COUNT(CustomerId) >= 15

data.question: What is the last name of the customers who shopped at the bakery more than 10 times?

prediction.sql: SELECT c.LastName FROM customers c JOIN receipts r ON c.Id = r.CustomerId GROUP BY c.Id HAVING COUNT(r.ReceiptNumber) > 10

data.question: How many types of Cake does this bakery sell?

prediction.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Food) FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cake')

data.question: List all the flavors of Croissant available in this bakery.

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Croissant')

data.question: Give me a list of all the distinct items bought by the customer number 15.

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Item FROM Item AS T1 INNER JOIN Ordinal AS T2 ON T1.Ordinal = T2.Ordinal INNER JOIN Receipt AS T3 ON T2.ReceiptNumber = T3.ReceiptNumber WHERE T3.CustomerId = 15

data.question: For each type of food, what are the average, maximum and minimum price?

prediction.sql: SELECT Food, AVG(Price) AS avg_price, MAX(Price) AS max_price, MIN(Price) AS min_price FROM goods GROUP BY Food

data.question: Find the receipt numbers where both Cake and Cookie were bought.

prediction.sql: SELECT Receipt FROM Item WHERE Id IN (SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cake') OR LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cookie'))

data.question: Find all the receipt numbers in which customer id 5 purchased Croissant.

prediction.sql: SELECT T2.ReceiptNumber FROM goods AS T1 INNER JOIN receipts AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.CustomerId WHERE LOWER(T1.Food) = LOWER('Croissant') AND T1.Id = 5

data.question: What is the receipt number and date of the receipt in which the most expensive item was bought?

prediction.sql: SELECT r.ReceiptNumber, r.Date FROM receipts AS r INNER JOIN items AS i ON r.ReceiptNumber = i.Receipt INNER JOIN goods AS g ON i.Ordinal = g.Id ORDER BY g.Price DESC LIMIT 1

data.question: What is the item that was bought the least number of times?

prediction.sql: SELECT i.Item FROM Receipt r INNER JOIN Ordinal o ON r.ReceiptID = o.ReceiptID INNER JOIN Item i ON o.OrdinalID = i.OrdinalID GROUP BY i.Item ORDER BY COUNT(i.Item) ASC LIMIT 1

data.question: How many goods are available for each food type?

prediction.sql: SELECT Food, COUNT(*) FROM goods GROUP BY Food

data.question: What is the average price for each food type?

prediction.sql: SELECT Food, AVG(Price) FROM goods GROUP BY Food

prediction.sql: SELECT * FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Flavor) = LOWER('Apricot') AND Price < 5

data.question: Find flavor of cakes that cost more than 10 dollars.

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE Price > 10

prediction.sql: SELECT * FROM goods WHERE Id LIKE '%APP%'

data.question: Find all the items that have chocolate flavor but were not bought more than 10 times.

prediction.sql: SELECT i.* FROM items AS i INNER JOIN Receipt AS r ON i.Id = r.ItemId INNER JOIN Ordinal AS o ON r.Ordinal = o.Ordinal WHERE LOWER(i.Flavor) = LOWER('chocolate') AND o.Ordinal <= 10

data.question: What is the three most popular goods in this bakery?

prediction.sql: SELECT i.Name, COUNT(o.Ordinal) AS Count FROM Receipt r INNER JOIN Ordinal o ON r.ReceiptID = o.ReceiptID INNER JOIN Item i ON o.ItemID = i.ItemID GROUP BY i.Name ORDER BY Count DESC LIMIT 3
